title: Chapter 339
content:
Chapter 339: Chapter 339 Your Mum’s Pussy Is The Best Pussy

Stormzy’s face twisted the second Dickson’s words registered. His hand moved fast, yanking a pistol from the back of his waist and pointing it straight at Dickson’s head.

"What the fuck did you just say?" Stormzy’s voice was low, dangerous, shaking with pure rage.

Dickson’s heart jumped to his throat, his eyes widening at the sight of the black steel aimed right at his face. In that instant, his entire fake bravado collapsed. He raised both hands like a coward caught red-handed.

"Wait, wait, bro, don’t take it the wrong way!" Dickson said quickly, sweat instantly forming at his temple. "I didn’t mean it like that. What I meant is—your mum’s pussy is the best pussy!"

Silence fell.

Even Stormzy froze, his finger still tense on the trigger. His eyes blinked once, twice, trying to process the absolute stupidity he just heard. His guards stared blankly, unsure if this fool was insane or suicidal.

Kelly slapped her forehead so hard she almost left a red mark. Vanessa just closed her eyes, muttering a curse under her breath.

Stormzy’s jaw worked as he clenched his teeth, still holding the gun level. He looked like he was deciding whether to pull the trigger or not.

"You..." Stormzy finally said, his voice low with venom. "You’re fucking dead."

He raised the gun slightly higher, ready to blow Dickson’s brains out.

"WAIT!" Kelly shouted, stepping forward quickly before Stormzy lost his control. She pushed her hands out. "Please, please—don’t waste your bullet on him! He’s an idiot with a big mouth. That’s it. Nothing else. He doesn’t mean what he says."

Stormzy’s eyes didn’t leave Dickson. His finger tightened just a little more on the trigger.

"Please," Kelly pressed on, her tone serious but respectful. "We’re counting on you for this deal. Don’t let him ruin it. I’ll control him. Just ignore him."

Stormzy stayed still for a moment, lips twitching as he struggled to contain his anger. Finally, with a frustrated growl, he lowered the gun and shoved it back into his waistband. His eyes cut through Dickson like knives.

"You better watch your fucking dog," he spat, turning back to Kelly and Vanessa.

Vanessa gave a stiff nod, forcing a polite smile even though inside she wanted to strangle Dickson herself.

Stormzy motioned with his hand, and his guards immediately moved toward the two cars parked in front. "Let’s move out!" he barked.

Before following, Stormzy turned his head one last time. His glare landed on Dickson, who still had his hands up like a kid caught stealing candy. "If your stupid driver can’t keep up," Stormzy growled, "that’s it. Don’t bother coming."

Then he slipped into his car, slamming the door. His guards followed, sliding into the second car. Both engines roared alive, and within seconds, the two vehicles pulled out of the lot, speeding down the road.

Vanessa and Kelly wasted no time. They turned and rushed back to the SUV. Kelly climbed into the passenger side, Vanessa into the back. The second their doors shut, they both snapped at Dickson in unison.

"Drive!"

Dickson blinked at them, his hands still frozen on the wheel. "What, like now?"

"YES, NOW, YOU FUCKING IDIOT!" Vanessa yelled, nearly climbing over the seat to smack him.

Kelly’s voice was sharper, cutting like a blade. "If you don’t move right now, this mission is over. Step on it!"

That was all Dickson needed to hear. A grin spread across his face, a reckless glint flashing in his eyes.

"Ohhhh," he said, cracking his knuckles. "So you’re telling me to speed, huh? That’s all I needed to hear!"

He slapped the gearshift into drive, his foot slamming down on the accelerator. The SUV roared to life, the tires screeching against the asphalt as the massive vehicle lunged forward.

The force of acceleration threw Vanessa back into her seat, and Kelly grabbed the dashboard instantly, her knuckles turning white.

"Dickson!" Kelly screamed. "Don’t be a fool—just keep it steady!"

But Dickson wasn’t listening. He leaned forward, eyes wide with excitement as the SUV surged down the road. The engine growled, and the speedometer climbed rapidly.

"Ohhh yeah!" Dickson shouted over the roar. "Finally, they told me to drive recklessly! This is what I was born for!"

The SUV weaved violently, swerving between cars on the busy morning street. Horns blared, drivers cursed, but Dickson only laughed like a maniac.

"You see that gap?" Dickson yelled, pointing between two cars. "Most people would slow down—but not me!"

He yanked the wheel, squeezing the massive SUV through a space barely wide enough. The side mirror clipped the edge of another car, sending the driver honking wildly, but Dickson didn’t even flinch.

Kelly slapped his arm. "Do you want to get us killed?!"

"Relax!" Dickson shouted back. "This is premium driving, baby! Formula One level!"

Vanessa leaned forward, her voice sharp. "You stupid bastard, keep your eyes on the target cars or I’ll choke you with this seatbelt!"

Dickson just grinned wider. "Don’t worry, don’t worry. I’ve got them locked in!"

Up ahead, Stormzy’s two cars sped smoothly, cutting through the street with precision. They didn’t seem to notice the SUV barreling behind them like a mad bull.

But what Stormzy’s men didn’t see was the sleek black supercar following at a distance.

It hugged the SUV’s shadow, perfectly aligning itself so that the bulk of Dickson’s reckless driving acted as cover. Every time the SUV swerved, the supercar shifted smoothly, its tinted windows hiding the two women inside.

Inside, Lilith sat calm, her sharp eyes locked on the road. Seo Yeon sat nervously beside her, both hands gripping her seatbelt tight. The silence inside the supercar contrasted with the chaos of the SUV ahead.

Lilith’s fingers tapped lightly on the steering wheel. She didn’t need to rush. She didn’t need to panic. Every move was calculated, her car blending seamlessly into the background as though invisible.

Finally, Lilith raised a hand to her ear, tapping the small comm device.

"Liam, are you there?" Her voice was calm, collected.

Static. No answer.

She tried again, her voice a little sharper. "Liam. Respond."

Still nothing but silence.

Her jaw clenched slightly, though her expression remained controlled. She tapped the comm one more time.

"Liam. Answer me."

The line was dead.

"What the hell is he doing?"
